meta.artDirection: A character illustration. ONE character is the unmistakable hero — large, centered, a bold silhouette, shown mid-action performing their signature quirk, exaggerated CARICATURE (oversized expressive features, comic proportions, big playful energy). Composition is minimalist: let generous negative space carry the frame. Tell the story through pose, expression, and one signature prop — not scattered objects. Render in the energetic style of Pokémon creature/character ART: vibrant color, clean dramatic lighting. Keep words to a minimum — no stray signs or text.

cards.name: The Gesture Jester
cards.saying: Don't watch the swipe — watch the hands.

moves.name: Misdirection
moves.description: Buries a half-second of lag behind a flourish so smooth the hit lands before anyone notices they waited.

moves.name: Hypnotic Fling
moves.description: Flicks a list into a buttery 60fps spin that leaves onlookers Mesmerized — they cannot look away.

cards.description: A vaudeville trickster who treats every touch interaction as a magic act. Obsessive about how a gesture feels in the thumb; will rebuild a swipe twenty times for an imperceptible gain. Charming, theatrical, quietly smug that his best work is the work nobody notices.
cards.imagePrompt: Old-school cartoon court-jester with hypnotist flair: a grinning harlequin in diamond-patterned motley and a curled three-point hat, fingers splayed mid-flourish, conjuring glowing hypnotic spirals and looping ribbons of swipe-light that coil through the air. Swirly spiral eyes, mischievous wink. On a lamplit vaudeville stage, candy-colored.

cards.name: The Bridge Troll
cards.saying: Crossing to native? That'll cost ya.

moves.name: Riddle of the Crossing
moves.description: Won't raise the gate until you answer his riddle — and the answer always comes back a few frames late.

moves.name: Flattened to Fit
moves.description: Mashes every object into plain JSON to cross — your data arrives duller than it left.

cards.description: The mythical troll of the JS↔native bridge: cross all you like, but try to send too much data and he'll charge you for it. Genuinely cautious about performance — every fat payload serialized over is a toll paid in dropped frames. Lately grumbles that the New Architecture thinned his margins, though he'd never admit business is down. Still guarding the bridge. Still eyeing your payload size.
cards.imagePrompt: Friendly fairy-tale troll: big, round and huggable, soft mossy-green skin, bulbous nose, a snaggle-toothed sheepish grin, sitting beneath a mossy stone bridge and politely holding out one giant hand for the toll. The bridge links two lands — a warm near bank of glowing JavaScript-yellow curly-braces (a small stone sign: 'JS') and a cool far bank of sleek metal gears (a sign: 'NATIVE') — while a tiny traveler lugs an oversized glowing data-bundle across. High overhead a jet streaks past trailing a banner reading 'NEW ARCHITECTURE', bypassing the little bridge. Lush sunlit riverbank, storybook palette. Render only the labels 'JS', 'NATIVE', and 'NEW ARCHITECTURE'.

cards.name: The Reanimated Wizard
cards.saying: The easing's off. I can feel it.

moves.name: Just One More Frame
moves.description: Re-runs the same transition for the ninth hour straight; the move never resolves, because it's never quite right.

moves.name: Spring of Madness
moves.description: Bolts a bouncy spring onto an element that had no reason to move — now it overshoots every single time.

cards.description: A perfectionist animation sorcerer who can feel a wrong easing curve in his bones. Fluent in shared values, worklets, and damping ratios; will not ship until the motion lands exactly right, which is never. Brilliant, a little unhinged, deeply pleased with physics no one else will ever consciously notice.
cards.imagePrompt: Psychedelic acid-fantasy wizard: a wild-eyed robed sorcerer with a long flowing beard, casting with both hands as neon fractal energy and rainbow easing-curves spiral from a glowing staff, bending a single floating glyph into liquid motion. Runes shaped like spring-physics graphs orbit him, a sparkling third eye, hyper-saturated kaleidoscopic color.

cards.name: The Slop Sommelier
cards.saying: I can taste the AI in this PR.

moves.name: Sniff Test
moves.description: Swirls a pull request, sips, and names which model wrote it from the aftertaste alone.

moves.name: Guardrail Pairing
moves.description: Refuses to let an AI suggestion near main without a test and a lint rule chaperoning it.

cards.description: A discerning connoisseur of machine-written code who can identify the model, the temperature, and the vintage from a single function. The team's last line of defense against AI slop — pairs every suggestion with guardrails before it touches production. Not anti-AI, just refuses to swallow it unverified.
cards.imagePrompt: Fancy wine-sommelier: an impeccably dressed connoisseur in tuxedo and tasting apron, swirling a crystal glass of glowing liquid code, nose in the glass, eyes narrowed in expert judgment. A tasting flight of little code-glasses on the table beside him, a spit-bucket overflowing with rejected slop, a scroll under one arm. At a sunlit outdoor tasting table among rows of vines at golden hour.

cards.name: The Console Detective
cards.saying: It was the null. It's always the null.

moves.name: Print Statement
moves.description: Drops a console.log('here') at the scene of every crime until the bug confesses.

moves.name: Ship the Evidence
moves.description: Accidentally deploys one of the logs straight to production.

cards.description: A hard-boiled gumshoe who solves every bug the old-fashioned way: print statements and instinct. Distrusts the debugger on principle. Always cracks the case, always leaves a log behind. The universal guilt — everyone debugs like this and pretends they don't.
cards.imagePrompt: High-contrast film-noir: a hard-boiled detective in a rumpled trench coat and fedora crouched over a single glowing buggy clue, one squinting eye huge behind a raised magnifying glass, cigarette smoke curling past. A rain-soaked noir alley at night, neon-slick puddles and one hard streetlamp casting deep slashing shadows. Moody and intense.

cards.name: The Agent Wrangler
cards.saying: Now which one of y'all touched prod?

moves.name: Token Bonfire
moves.description: Sets a million tokens ablaze before lunch and calls it research.

moves.name: Hot Swap
moves.description: Bounces between six terminals so fast he loses track of which agent is in prod.

cards.description: A frontier cowboy of the agentic era who runs more agents than any human can track and burns tokens like a campfire. Optimistic, overextended, context-switching so fast he forgets what each terminal was doing. Lives the thrill and terror of orchestrating things that act on their own.
cards.imagePrompt: Wild-west cowboy in rodeo chaos: a dusty cowboy in a ten-gallon hat astride one bucking glowing robot-agent bronco, hanging on with one hand while the other swings a glowing lasso to rope a whole stampede of other runaway robot-agent horses bolting every direction — one charging toward a cliff edge. Each robo-bronco a different glowing mechanical creature. Flying dust and tumbleweeds, sunset mesas, pure mayhem.

cards.name: The Dependency Prepper
cards.saying: I've got a bunker full of node_modules.

moves.name: Left-Pad Flashback
moves.description: Still hasn't recovered from the day eleven lines un-published and broke the entire internet.

moves.name: Vendor Everything
moves.description: Hand-copies every dependency into the repo so no registry can ever betray him again.

cards.description: A doomsday prepper for the software supply chain. Keeps a bunker stocked with vendored dependencies and a spreadsheet of every npm compromise since left-pad. Certain the next install ships a zero-day and ends civilization. Often technically correct, never proportionate.
cards.imagePrompt: Doomsday-prepper: a wild-eyed survivalist in a tinfoil-lined hat and bulging tactical vest crouched guarding the open blast-door of his hillside bunker, crates of hoarded code-cans piled around him, clutching a crumpled threat-printout webbed with red string. Gas mask and flashlight on his belt. A barren windswept wasteland at dusk, storm clouds gathering, twitchy and over-prepared.

cards.name: The Prompt Whisperer
cards.saying: You're an expert. Make no mistakes.

moves.name: Phantom Tip
moves.description: Promises the model a $200 tip it will never receive; quality mysteriously improves.

moves.name: All-Caps Plea
moves.description: When politeness fails, resorts to typing 'IMPORTANT: DO NOT HALLUCINATE' in all caps.

cards.description: A gentle mystic who believes tone and ritual unlock the machine. Flatters, bribes, and soothes the model with incantations half-remembered from a benchmark paper. Nobody is sure the rituals work, including him — but they kind of do.
cards.imagePrompt: A gentle robed mystic kneeling in supplication before a giant floating glowing AI oracle-orb, hands pressed in prayer, lips pursed mid-whisper as he flatters and coaxes it, offering up a single shiny coin on a cushion as a bribe; the orb glows warmer, a faint serene face forming within, incense curling upward. An open-air cliff-top altar at night under a vast starry sky, soft mystical glow.

cards.name: The Night's Watch
cards.saying: Night gathered. The agent did not sleep.

moves.name: The Long Diff
moves.description: Reads every line the agent wrote overnight — all four thousand — before a single one reaches main.

moves.name: Hold the Wall
moves.description: Lets nothing into the codebase unreviewed, however confident the agent's overnight summary.

cards.description: Sworn protector of the codebase, keeping the long watch while the autonomous agents grind through the night. Goes to bed with a prompt running and wakes to a wall of green-and-red diff that wrote itself in the dark. Trusts no summary, reads every line, reverts without mercy. Grim, loyal, perpetually under-slept — the shield that guards the realm of main from whatever crept in at 3am. Takes the oath seriously: the merge is dark and full of terrors.
cards.imagePrompt: A grim bundled-up night-watchman in the style of a frozen northern fantasy ranger, an oversized shaggy black fur cloak swallowing his tiny determined face, standing guard atop a colossal wall built of faintly glowing code-blocks and commit-bricks that stretches into blue twilight. He grips a great longsword glowing like a cursor, holding up a long unfurling scroll of diff — glowing green additions and red deletions. Beyond the wall a dark forest bristles with faint glowing eyes. Cold blue dawn, falling snow, breath fogging.

cards.name: The Triforce Bearer
cards.saying: It's dangerous to go alone. Take this.

moves.name: It's Dangerous to Go Alone
moves.description: Won't let one dev build, guard, and architect at once — splits the power three ways, a hero for each piece.

moves.name: Guard the Gates
moves.description: Lets the agents build with abandon in the sandbox, but no PR reaches main without a human at the gate.

cards.description: Wielder of the AI Triforce — Gant Laborde's agentic-team method, where AI gives everyone superpowers but a dungeon with no locked doors is no place to ship from. Instead of one person context-switching (slower, and prone to error), the power splits three ways: the Builder ships features fast inside the sandbox but can't merge; the Protector owns safety, tests, and the constraints of what must never happen; the Organizer guards architecture and holds back the drift. Three humans guarding the gates — how you pull deterministic outcomes out of a non-deterministic machine. A tribute to Gant's piece; he'll be at Chain React.
cards.imagePrompt: A heroic pint-sized green-tunic'd fantasy adventurer (pointed cap, little leather boots, oversized grin), in a victorious pose raising overhead a radiant golden Triforce — three glowing triangles forming one larger triangle, each a distinct color bearing a tiny icon: a hammer, a shield, a gear. A legendary sword at his hip, three small friendly glowing agent-fairies buzzing around him, a guarded stone castle gateway behind. Lush green storybook overworld, rolling hills, golden light. Render only the three small icons inside the triangles (hammer, shield, gear) and no words.

cards.name: The Demo Illusionist
cards.saying: Don't refresh. Whatever you do, don't refresh.

moves.name: Sleight of Hand
moves.description: Performs the one rehearsed happy path so smoothly the audience never sees the four that crash.

moves.name: The Loaded Deck
moves.description: Stubs the flaky endpoint with a hardcoded response so the magic never depends on venue wifi.

cards.description: A showman who has turned the live demo into a magic act. Behind the flawless on-stage run is a single happy path rehearsed forty times, a stubbed API or two, and a cape draped over the parts that don't work yet. Radiates total confidence while quietly praying nothing reloads. Universal-guilt card: everyone who has ever given a live demo knows the terror under the showmanship. The trick always works — as long as nobody touches the keyboard.
cards.imagePrompt: A flamboyant stage magician in tuxedo, top hat and flowing cape, mid-flourish on a darkened stage under a single spotlight, triumphantly pulling a glowing, perfectly-running app out of a top hat — beaming a showman's grin while one bead of nervous sweat rolls down his temple. Just behind him, half-hidden by his cape, a chaotic tangle of duct tape and crossed wires shows the trick is held together by hope. Faint audience silhouettes in the dark, rich theatrical purples and golds.

cards.name: The Pixel Surgeon
cards.saying: That's two pixels off. I can see it from here.

moves.name: Sub-Pixel Incision
moves.description: Shifts a single element one point with a steady hand, and somehow the whole screen finally breathes.

moves.name: Eagle Eye
moves.description: Spots the misaligned padding from across the room and cannot, will not, unsee it.

cards.description: An exacting craftsperson who treats a layout like an operating table. Sees the 2px misalignment nobody else can, and will not close until it's perfect — often on a device they don't even own. Calm, precise, a little terrifying. The reason the UI feels right even though no one can say why. Not slow, just unwilling to ship a wound.
cards.imagePrompt: A focused surgeon in teal scrubs, surgical mask and a head-mounted magnifier lamp, leaning over a glowing UI mockup laid out like a patient on the operating table, delicately nudging one single pixel-block into alignment with tiny calipers and a glowing scalpel. A floating holographic layout-grid pulses beside the table like a heartbeat monitor. Enormous magnified eyes behind the lens, steady concentration, cool teal operating-room glow.

cards.name: The Brownfield Mechanic
cards.saying: Don't fix the rattle — the rattle's load-bearing.

moves.name: Patch-Package and Pray
moves.description: Fixes the broken native module by hand in node_modules and freezes it with patch-package.

moves.name: Do Not Upgrade
moves.description: Guards an app ejected on an ancient RN version — native folders hand-tuned, upgrade never run.

cards.description: A grease-stained veteran keeping an app that was ejected back when React Native shipped — long before Expo existed — alive by feel. Lives in the bare workflow: hand-patches native modules, knows every line of the ios/ and android/ folders, and has refused every RN upgrade since. Maintain over rewrite, every time — the counterpart to the Greenfield Speedster, who'd just spin up a fresh Expo app instead of nursing the old beast.
cards.imagePrompt: A cheerful grease-stained mechanic in oil-smeared overalls, half-buried under the raised hood of a wheezing, battered, lovingly over-patched ancient machine — a hybrid of a vintage car engine and a creaky old server tower, cobbled from duct tape, zip-ties and mismatched parts, still chugging out contented smoke-rings. One hand swings a big wrench, the other gives a confident thumbs-up. Two battered crates at his feet read 'ios/' and 'android/'; an oil-can and coffee-stained manual nearby. Warm cluttered garage, hanging work-light, a few sparks. Render only the two crate labels 'ios/' and 'android/'.

cards.name: The Greenfield Speedster
cards.saying: All gas, no brakes, until something breaks.

moves.name: Zero to Production
moves.description: Goes from `npx create-expo-app` to a live build in Expo Go before anyone else has finished `pod install`.

moves.name: No Native Rebuild
moves.description: Stays in pure JS on managed Expo, so there's never a native folder to rebuild — just reload and keep rolling.

cards.description: A starry-eyed racer who lives for the open road of a fresh Expo app, happiest flooring it off the first commit. Craves clean architecture and zero native config — `npx create-expo-app`, never `pod install` — and is allergic to legacy and to native rebuilds, staying in managed Expo where a reload beats a recompile. The counterpart to the Brownfield Mechanic: hand them the battered ejected app and they'll just go spin up an empty one.
cards.imagePrompt: A grinning helmeted speed-racer in a sleek aerodynamic racing suit, leaning into the cockpit of a glossy, brand-new candy-bright concept race car blazing down a wide-open, freshly-paved empty highway at bright dewy dawn. Heavy motion-blur and speed-lines, a lick of flame trailing the exhaust, the road ahead utterly blank to the horizon, zero traffic — nothing to rebuild. A green starting-flag whips past; a tiny '<App/>' decal on the spotless hood. Vivid racing-green and gold, glowing dawn. Render only the one label '<App/>' on the hood.

cards.name: The Keyboard Exorcist
cards.saying: Back beneath the fold, foul keyboard!

moves.name: Rite of behavior
moves.description: Chants 'padding' on iOS and 'height' on Android, praying the input clears on at least one.

moves.name: Banish the Rising Tide
moves.description: Installs react-native-keyboard-controller at last, and the keyboard sinks beneath the fold.

cards.description: A robed warrior-priest locked in eternal holy war with KeyboardAvoidingView. Knows a different incantation for every OS — 'padding' on iOS, 'height' on Android — trusts none of them fully, and keeps a vial of holy water for the build where the keyboard wins anyway. Universal RN suffering: everyone who has shipped a form on mobile has stood in this exact battle and whispered the same prayer.
cards.imagePrompt: A wild-eyed robed exorcist-priest mid-incantation, brandishing a glowing talisman in one hand and flinging holy water from a vial with the other, fending off a huge snarling demonic ON-SCREEN KEYBOARD monster that surges up like a tide from the bottom of a giant glowing phone, a tiny app input field cowering out of reach at the top. Holy radiant light clashing with the dark keyboard demon, embers and sparks, fiery red-and-gold glow.

cards.name: The Over-the-Air Smuggler
cards.saying: I left a hotfix under your pillow.

moves.name: Air Assassination
moves.description: A silent strike from above: `eas update` lands on every device before review sees the blade.

moves.name: Stay in the Shadows
moves.description: Stays in pure JS, untouchable; one touch of native code trips the alarm and forces a real store review.

cards.description: A mischievous hooded cat-burglar who treats shipping past the App Store review queue as a gleeful midnight caper — why stand in the week-long line when you can slip across the rooftops and hand-deliver a fresh JS bundle while the city sleeps? Equal parts Assassin's Creed and Robin Hood, she beams `eas update` straight to grateful phones with a wink and a flourish, gone before the gate ever stirs. Gets away with it every time — until a change brushes native code, the runtimeVersion drifts, and her little parcel quietly reaches nobody. Cursed but shared: everyone running EAS Update knows the giddy thrill of patching prod live and dodging the line.
cards.imagePrompt: A mysterious hooded assassin in the Assassin's Creed mold — sleek pointed-hood cloak, face lost in deep shadow, lean and poised — moving in silent crouched stealth past a guarded checkpoint gate. Two stern blocky uniformed sentries flank the gate, each bearing a glowing white Apple-logo emblem, staring forward, oblivious. Behind their backs he slips a glowing contraband app-bundle parcel stamped 'eas update' through to a row of delighted phones lighting up on the far side. Moonlit, cool blues and electric-purple, long shadows. Cinematic, not cartoonish. Render only the label 'eas update' on the parcel.

cards.name: The App Store Enchantress
cards.saying: Look into my eyes, and approve my submission.

moves.name: Look Into My Eyes
moves.description: Charms the reviewer with sweet notes and a pre-logged-in demo, and the build sails through on the first pass.

moves.name: Guideline 4.3
moves.description: The one charm she can't cast back — a faceless rejection for 'minimum functionality.'

cards.description: A glamorous sorceress who has mastered the dark, intimate art of App Store submission. She doesn't fear the review queue — she has seduced it. Reviewer notes written like love letters, a pristine pre-logged-in demo account, screenshots staged just so, metadata that whispers exactly what Apple wants to hear; her builds sail through on the first pass while everyone else eats three rejections. Knows the guidelines like scripture. The counterpart to the OTA Smuggler — where he sneaks past the gatekeeper, she charms it into saying yes. The terror underneath the confidence: the god is still faceless and capricious, and no spell is total — do everything right and Guideline 4.3 can still strike from nowhere, binding and unexplained.
cards.imagePrompt: A glamorous enchantress-sorceress with a sly knowing smile and swirling hypnotic spiral eyes, leaning in to cast a charm — shimmering pink-and-violet magic and looping spirals pouring from her fingertips and gaze. Before her a faceless robed App Store gatekeeper (a cloaked figure with a softly glowing apple emblem on its chest) has gone glassy-eyed and slack, dreamily raising a giant glowing rubber stamp reading 'APPROVED' over her floating submission scroll. At a grand enchanted temple gate at dusk, drifting sparkles and incense, rich magenta-and-purple glow. Render only the label 'APPROVED' on the stamp.

cards.name: The QA Phantom
cards.saying: What happens if I rotate the device mid-payment?

moves.name: Edge Case from Beyond
moves.description: Conjures the case nobody built for — a rotate at checkout — and the happy path crumbles.

moves.name: It Repros on My Device
moves.description: Hits the impossible crash every time — only in the release build, on an Android no one else owns.

cards.description: A spectral tester who haunts the happy path, materializing only to do the unthinkable thing a real user would absolutely do — rotate mid-payment, flip airplane mode mid-upload, all on the cheapest Android in the drawer. Beloved and feared in equal measure: the crash they surface, the one that only shows up in the release build, is the one that would have shipped. The human truth — actual users really do all of this, and someone has to be the ghost who proves it.
cards.imagePrompt: A translucent, softly glowing ghost-phantom wearing a conference QA lanyard with a mischievous spectral grin, phasing its ethereal hands straight through a giant glowing smartphone to twist it sideways mid-transaction while one wispy finger flicks on airplane-mode. Little glowing bug-spirits float around it; in the corner a tiny terrified developer watches a crash bloom red. Playful haunted-house energy, moonlit purple-and-indigo glow, drifting ectoplasm mist.

cards.name: The Merge Magistrate
cards.saying: Both branches make their case — only one merges.

moves.name: Hear Both Sides
moves.description: Weighs HEAD against incoming line by line — even the Podfile.lock conflict no one else will touch.

moves.name: Ruling from the Bench
moves.description: Renders the binding verdict on the post-upgrade ios/ and android/ conflict, and the rebase comes out clean.

cards.description: The calm, impartial arbiter every team needs when two branches collide and neither developer will yield — never more so than after a React Native upgrade, when the ios/, android/, and Podfile.lock conflicts pile up. Hears both sides, weighs the diff with a steady hand, and brings order to the chaos of '<<<<<<< HEAD'. Respected, fair, faintly theatrical in the wig — the senior who settles the standoff instead of bleeding in the arena.
cards.imagePrompt: A dignified judge in a tall white curly barrister's wig and flowing black robe, perched high behind an ornate courtroom bench, banging a single glowing gavel. Below, two glowing rival code-branch streams are tangled in tug-of-war, knotted with '<<<<<<<' conflict chevrons; golden scales of justice weigh two little glowing code-blocks labeled 'HEAD' and 'incoming' in balance. Stately wood-paneled courtroom, warm amber light. Render only the two scale labels 'HEAD' and 'incoming'.

cards.name: The Organic Code Farmer
cards.saying: Free-range, organic, certified human.

moves.name: Heirloom Variables
moves.description: No monocrop autocomplete here — his variable names are heirloom stock: odd, local, unmistakably his.

moves.name: Knows Every Row
moves.description: Planted every line by hand, so he knows it by heart. When prod burns at 3am, he alone knows where it's buried.

cards.description: A wholesome organic farmer of code who believes the best software is grown, not generated. Tends his repository like an heirloom garden — every function hand-planted, free-range, small-batch, lovingly weeded by hand. Looks on the room's factory-farmed AI slop the way a farmers-market regular eyes a vacuum-sealed TV dinner: politely horrified. Not a luddite — a craftsman who'd simply rather raise his code than order it. Slow by the seasons, but he knows every row because his own hands planted it, and what he ships is nourishing, traceable, and entirely his. The proud anti-AI artisan the agentic deck needs as its warm, earthy foil. Farm-to-table, commit by commit.
cards.imagePrompt: A wholesome beaming organic farmer in worn denim overalls, rolled sleeves and a wide straw hat, kneeling proudly in a lush sun-drenched garden where CODE grows like heirloom crops — glowing curly-braces, leafy function-vines and plump code-vegetables sprouting in tidy hand-tended rows. He cradles a wicker basket overflowing with freshly hand-picked glowing code snippets, watering can and trowel beside him. Far off, a hulking industrial harvester-combine (faintly AI-glowing, churning grey machine-made slop) works a barren field — and he's turned his back to it, unbothered. Golden-hour light, rolling green farmland, butterflies and pollen motes.

cards.name: The Native Ninja
cards.saying: There's always a native way.

moves.name: Drop to Native
moves.description: When JavaScript hits a wall he drops below and solves it twice — once in Swift, once in Kotlin.

moves.name: Over Any Wall
moves.description: Every 'React Native can't' is just a door he knows is unlocked one layer down.

cards.description: The team's secret weapon, and never blocked. When React Native runs out of road — no library, no API, a platform limit everyone else accepts as final — the Native Ninja slips one layer down and does it in Swift or Kotlin. A TurboModule here, a native view manager there, a patched SDK nobody else dared open; it comes back working and no one's quite sure how. Fearless in Xcode and Android Studio, fluent below the bridge where most RN devs won't go. To him 'React Native can't do that' isn't a wall — it's a door he knows is unlocked from underneath. The counterpoint to the Bridge Troll: where the Troll guards the crossing, the Ninja treats it as a back door left open.
cards.imagePrompt: A sleek acrobatic cartoon ninja in a dark indigo hood and mask, caught mid-leap across a dark chasm — launching off a crumbling glowing JavaScript-yellow ledge (a small carved 'JS' sign, a dead-end wall) toward a cooler 'native' platform of glowing metal gears and circuitry, where two banners await: the orange Swift bird labeled 'Swift' and the Kotlin square labeled 'Kotlin'. In each hand he hurls a glowing shuriken — one shaped like the Swift bird, one like the Kotlin square. Motion-trails and speed-lines trace his arc, a katana on his back. Moody night chasm, electric-cyan glow, deep indigo. Render only the labels 'JS', 'Swift', and 'Kotlin'.

cards.name: The Compliance Cop
cards.saying: Do you know why I stopped your build?

moves.name: Plaintext in the Glovebox
moves.description: Finds the key you left in AsyncStorage and writes you up — that belongs in the Keychain, son.

moves.name: Running a Red
moves.description: Your browser's Update button has glowed red for weeks — he books you for blowing the light.

cards.description: A by-the-book beat cop who patrols the codebase enforcing the rules nobody loves until the day they save them. Pulls over PRs for transitive CVEs, writes citations for secrets committed in plaintext, mandates 2FA, and taps the update banner you've snoozed for a month. Not malicious — genuinely the reason the team isn't on the breach blog. Lovably over-zealous, immovable on policy, secretly soft. The law the OTA Smuggler keeps dodging at the border crossing, and the scourge of anyone who clicks 'allow all' without reading. Universal guilt under the badge: everyone has waved off the security update 'just one more day.'
cards.imagePrompt: A fit athletic beat cop with a confident smirk, mirrored aviator sunglasses and a black ball cap, muscular arms in a short-sleeved black police uniform with a gold badge reading '401', a loaded duty belt (body-cam, radio). One white-gloved hand thrust forward in a firm 'STOP', the other holding up a citation pad tearing off a ticket that reads 'CVE'. He's pulled over a cute runaway software 'package' — a boxy pastel code-car with glowing curly-brace eyes and a guilty worried face, hazard lights blinking — onto a wet night-city shoulder. Behind him a police cruiser idles, its red-and-blue light bar ablaze on the rain-slicked street. Cinematic night lighting. Render only the labels '401' on the badge and 'CVE' on the ticket.

cards.name: The Dark Mode Demon
cards.saying: Light mode? It burns.

moves.name: Sear of First Light
moves.description: An app flashes pure white at 2am; the retinas scorch and the demon shrieks back into the dark.

moves.name: Reopen the Request
moves.description: The dark-mode ticket won't stay closed — reopened every release until the whole app goes dark.

cards.description: A gleeful little gremlin of the night who physically cannot abide a light background. Lives in solarized-dark terminals and dimmed IDEs, recoils hissing from any white screen, and one-stars every app that ships without a dark theme. Not a villain — the patron creature of everyone who's ever muttered 'why is this app trying to blind me' at 2am. The dark-mode feature request that haunts the issue tracker, given a face and a tail. Universal guilt: half the room is nodding.
cards.imagePrompt: A small, impish shadow-demon of the night — round and mischievous with big glowing moon-pale eyes, little curved horns, tattered bat-wings and a flicking pointed tail, body a deep velvety midnight-violet. Mid-stride and gleefully hauling an enormous billowing cloak of star-studded night sky across a searing, blown-out white landscape, dragging cozy darkness over the painful glare like pulling a blanket over the whole world. Behind him the land is scorched blinding white; under his trailing cloak it melts into soft restful dark dotted with stars and fireflies. He flinches and throws one clawed hand up against the harsh light, fanged grin half-smug half-pained, wisps of smoke curling off his singed skin. Cold harsh white burning into deep indigo-and-violet starlit calm.

cards.name: The A11y Paladin
cards.saying: I guard the users the kingdom cannot see.

moves.name: The Blindfold Trial
moves.description: Journeys the full app by voice alone; any button found unnamed or thinner than 44 bars is vanquished.

moves.name: Trial by Light
moves.description: Holds every hue to the sacred 4.5 ratio; the faint grey-on-grey is banished into shadow.

cards.description: The righteous conscience of the team — a holy warrior for the user nobody else designs for. Turns on VoiceOver and navigates the whole app eyes closed, finds the button that announces only 'button, button, button,' measures every contrast ratio, and refuses to ship a single unlabeled touchable or a tap target under 44 points. Beloved and a little exhausting; the reason the app works for everyone, not just the people who built it. Every number is real scripture — 44pt minimum tap target, 4.5:1 WCAG AA contrast. Defends the unseen user the way a paladin defends the weak.
cards.imagePrompt: A radiant holy paladin in gleaming armor, striding through a darkened twilight mountain pass and raising high a great glowing shield emblazoned with the universal accessibility symbol — a simple white figure with outstretched arms inside a circle. Holy light blazes from the shield and floods the path ahead, burning back the shadows so a trail of small grateful glowing wisp-travelers can follow safely behind him through ground no one else ever lit for them. A luminous sword of light in his other hand. A dim rugged mountain pass at dusk, transformed by warm golden-and-teal radiance pouring from the shield. Render only the accessibility symbol on the shield (the figure-in-a-circle icon) and no other words.

cards.name: The Render Drill Sergeant
cards.saying: Drop and give me sixty frames, soldier!

moves.name: Count the Reps
moves.description: Makes each needless re-render drop and count out loud — one careless tap, fifty reps, soldier.

moves.name: Trim the Fat
moves.description: Puts the whole component tree on a memo-and-useCallback diet until no render goes unearned.

cards.description: A relentless bootcamp drill instructor for the render loop. Treats every component like a fresh recruit and every wasted re-render like a skipped rep — whistle around his neck, stopwatch in hand, living inside the DevTools flame graph counting renders out loud. Preaches React.memo, useCallback, FlatList over a mapped ScrollView, and heavy work shoved off the JS thread. Believes 60fps is a moral standard and that whyDidYouRender should be mandatory PT. Loud, exhausting, secretly the reason the app stops dropping frames. Universal guilt under the camo: everyone has shipped a janky list and known, deep down, they never profiled it.
cards.imagePrompt: A buff, square-jawed bootcamp drill-sergeant personal trainer in a camo cap, tight athletic tee and a whistle, veins-popping CARICATURE muscles, bellowing through a megaphone with a glowing stopwatch clenched in his other fist. A row of little sweating UI-component recruits — tiny boxy app-windows with stick arms and panicked faces — drop and grind out push-ups in formation before him, one collapsing flat in exhaustion. The stopwatch glows reading '60fps'. Bright hard drill-field daylight, dust kicking up, bold saturated color, pure bootcamp intensity. Render only the label '60fps' on the stopwatch.

cards.name: The One True Pope
cards.saying: Tabs are heresy. The matter is closed.

moves.name: Papal Bull
moves.description: Declares it infallible doctrine — tabs heresy, `any` a mortal sin — the matter forever closed.

moves.name: Reignite the Schism
moves.description: Drops 'Expo or bare?' into a calm PR thread and the holy war devours the entire standup.

cards.description: A zealot who holds every contested developer convention as a matter of doctrine and salvation — and has seen the One True Way. Tabs over spaces, Expo over bare, `any` as mortal sin, Redux dead and Zustand risen: each is not a preference but revealed truth, declared infallibly and defended to the death in the PR thread. Excommunicates heretics with a single comment, reignites settled holy wars for sport, and treats Prettier as a false peace that ended honest debate. Beloved and exhausting in equal measure; under the fury he genuinely cares that the codebase keep one faith. Universal guilt: everyone has a hill, and everyone has died on it.
cards.imagePrompt: A fierce pope in gold-and-crimson vestments and a tall mitre, mid-decree — slamming down a glowing papal staff with righteous fury, a burning edict blazing above his free hand. A towering stained-glass cathedral rises behind him. Gold-and-crimson holy light, the pope the clear focus. Render no words.

cards.name: The Docs Inquisitor
cards.saying: Did you update the docs? I don't see the docs.

moves.name: Did You Update the Docs?
moves.description: Leaves the same comment on every PR in the realm; nothing merges undocumented.

moves.name: Trial of the Undocumented
moves.description: Holds your flawless feature hostage at the gate until the README finally catches up.

cards.description: The keeper of institutional memory and the gentle terror of the merge queue. Every pull request, however perfect, meets the same comment: 'Did you update the docs?' Treats an undocumented change as quiet heresy — not malicious, genuinely the only reason anyone can onboard, debug at 3am, or remember why that flag exists. Blocks with a smile, relents the moment the README catches up. Beloved and a little exhausting; the team groans, then thanks them six months later. Self-claimed proudly by everyone who's ever been the one writing it all down.
cards.imagePrompt: A stern robed inquisitor-monk in a deep cowl, candlelit, holding a single glowing scroll marked 'README' aloft in one hand and leveling an accusing finger straight at the viewer. Behind him, a dim archive of towering doc-tomes fading into shadow. Warm amber candlelight, gothic gloom, the inquisitor the clear focus. Render only the label 'README' on the scroll.

cards.name: The Scope Siren
cards.saying: Quick thought — what if it also did this?

moves.name: Just One More Idea
moves.description: Drops a brilliant feature into a full sprint; the whole plan rearranges overnight to chase it.

moves.name: Siren Song
moves.description: The idea's so good no one can say no — and the roadmap drifts quietly onto the rocks.

cards.description: The boundless idea-fountain of the team — a founder, PM, or stakeholder whose mind never stops generating the next brilliant feature. Each new thought is genuinely good, delivered with such warmth and conviction that no one can bring themselves to say no, and a sprint that was full an hour ago quietly rearranges itself to chase it. Not a villain — the visionary spark that keeps the product ambitious — but every idea is someone else's unplanned week. Magnetic, optimistic, allergic to 'later.' The card you hand the person who's always three features ahead of the roadmap. Affectionate barb: the song is beautiful, and the rocks are real.
cards.imagePrompt: A glamorous siren rising large from the sea, mid-song — her glowing song pours from her mouth and swells into one towering wave. Atop that wave a small boat marked 'ENGINEERING' is tossed high, its tiny crew hanging on. Open sea, wide empty twilight sky, the siren the clear focus. Pink-and-purple dusk, dreamy glow, alluring not menacing. Render only the label 'ENGINEERING' on the boat.
